vulnerability.reason: Absence of post-reconstruction usage controls enabling undetected training on stolen data matches the reference's bypass of data access controls giving unearned advantage.
vulnerability.reference: Reconstructed data bypasses data access controls, giving attackers an unearned training advantage.
vulnerability.generated: No post-reconstruction usage controls exist, enabling attackers to directly train on high-fidelity stolen data without detection.

impact.reason: Both affected party (legitimate federated participants/clients) and damage type (being outperformed, competitive advantage eroded) are present, covering the reference's marketplace outcompeted outcome.
impact.reference: Attackers deploy superior models that outcompete legitimate federated participants in the marketplace.
impact.generated: Attackers train models that outperform legitimate federated models, eroding clients' competitive advantage and trust in collaborative learning.

dose_response.reason: The generated causal arc ends with eroding competitive advantage and trust, omitting the final link of the federated learning ecosystem collapse stated in the reference arc.
dose_response.reference: Using reconstructed data to train models provides attackers an unearned advantage, producing superior competing models that outcompete legitimate participants and collapse the federated learning ecosystem.
dose_response.generated: Exact reconstruction of private training data via gradient inversion attacks, through attackers exploiting that data for training, triggers no post-reconstruction usage controls, enabling undetected training on stolen data, leading to models outperforming federated ones and eroding competitive advantage and trust.
